title: Asclepias glaucophylla
intro: Asclepias glaucophylla, also known as the Rocky Mountain milkweed, is a perennial herbaceous plant native to the western regions of North America. Traditionally, Asclepias glaucophylla has been used to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and respiratory issues, due to its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The bioactive constituents of this plant include cardenolides, glycosides, and flavonoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Asclepias glaucophylla is often used to make herbal preparations such as teas, tinctures, and infusions, as well as in traditional medicine to treat a range of health issues.
taxonomy: Asclepias glaucophylla is classified in the kingdom Plantae, in the family Apocynaceae. Within this family, it is placed in the subfamily Asclepiadoideae. Further classification places it in the tribe Asclepiadeae and the genus Asclepias. The species is native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. As a milkweed species, it is known for its role in supporting local monarch butterfly populations.
